steps.title: Identify the grammatical elements
steps.content: ”Something new” is a noun phrase. “Sequencing the DNA found in Neanderthals' fossilized dental plaque” is a gerund phrase.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
A colon introduces an elaboration or appositive that clarifies a noun phrase.

Choice A correctly uses a colon to introduce “sequencing the DNA found in...” as an elaboration of “something new.” Choice B incorrectly uses a semicolon, which requires independent clauses on both sides, but “sequencing...” is a gerund phrase, not an independent clause; Choice C places the colon awkwardly after “sequencing”; and Choice D creates a sentence fragment by using a period.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
